message:
fix(stdio): ensure_host_libio_exit_safe before registry() initialisation

Ensures host libio exports are bootstrapped at least once before the
StreamRegistry singleton initialiser runs, so exit-time handlers
that touch host stdio symbols always see a consistent,
frankenlibc-aware view of the _IO_list_all chain.

crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs:

- New ensure_host_libio_exit_safe() idempotent guard: flips a new
  static HOST_LIBIO_EXIT_PATCHED AtomicBool with Ordering::AcqRel
  and, on the first caller, invokes
  io_internal_abi::bootstrap_host_libio_exports() to install the
  patched _IO_list_all head discovered by bd-faew. Subsequent
  calls become a single atomic compare-and-swap with no work.
- registry() now calls ensure_host_libio_exit_safe() at its entry,
  before the pointer-swap INIT / PTR dance. This means any code
  path that touches the stream registry (printf, fopen, freopen,
  fwrite, fclose, ...) also implicitly ensures the host libio
  exports are patched, which fixes a class of exit-time crashes
  where the host stdio atexit handler would run against the
  un-patched _IO_list_all and segfault after frankenlibc had
  already swapped in its own streams.

The atomic guard is placed ahead of the allocator-touching
Box::new for the Mutex so that on the very first registry()
call, the bootstrap runs in the small window before any
frankenlibc mutex is live - consistent with Finding 7 from the
Gemini review which flagged the Box::new recursion risk.
